mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 205) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.15 in same periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns in co-expansionary periods show negative or insignificant correlation (p > 0.1) with INDPRO/GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4.5%) increases wage pressures, prompting corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-unemployment regimes exceed 1.2% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.1 in same periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20d SPX returns in high-demand regimes show negative or insignificant correlation (p > 0.1) with RSAFS/HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals labor market strength, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-payroll growth periods show negative or insignificant correlation (p > 0.1) with PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST < 1900) signals weak demand, reducing construction activity and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns in low-housing-starts regimes exceed 1.8% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.1 in same periods.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing industrial production (INDPRO < 110) indicates supply-side weakness, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low-industrial-production periods show positive or insignificant correlation (p > 0.1) with INDPRO decline.
